name: Israel Cyber Network Defense Act
summary: Israel's Cyber Network Defense Act formalizes the Israel National Cyber Directorate's (INCD) authority to protect civilian cyberspace and critical infrastructure. It establishes legal powers for threat detection, incident response, and mandatory reporting, positioning Israel's cybersecurity framework among the most operationally active globally.
keyRequirements: Israel National Cyber Directorate (INCD) authority over civilian cybersecurity defense
Mandatory cybersecurity incident reporting for designated essential service organizations
Real-time threat intelligence sharing between INCD and critical infrastructure operators
INCD authority to issue binding cybersecurity directives during active cyber threats
Cybersecurity standards and guidelines for essential service sectors
National CERT (CERT-IL) coordination for civilian incident response and threat mitigation
penalties: Non-compliance with INCD directives may result in administrative enforcement measures and regulatory sanctions. Essential service organizations face corrective orders and mandatory remediation. Criminal penalties for cybercrimes addressed under existing computer crime statutes.
